chapter.heading: Early Warning Mechanism to prevent diversion of client securities

parent_section.heading: Early Warning Mechanism to prevent diversion of client securities
parent_section.full_text:
17. Early Warning Mechanism to prevent diversion of client securities23 
 
17.1 It has been decided to put in place an Early Warning Mechanism and sharing 
of information between Stock Exchanges, Depositories and Clearing 
Corporations to detect the diversion of client's securities by the stock broker at 
an early stage so as to take appropriate preventive measures. The threshold 
for such early warning signals shall be decide d by the Stock Exchanges, 
Depositories and Clearing Corporations with mutual consultation. 
 
17.2 Early warning signals, for prevention of diversion of clients' securities, may 
include the following: 
 
17.2.1 Deterioration in financial health of the stock broker/ deposi tory 
participant based on any of the following parameters: 
a) Significant reduction in net worth over previous half-year /year. 
b) Significant losses in the previous half years / years. 
c) Delay in reporting of Annual Report, Balance Sheet, Internal Audit 
Reports, Risk Based Supervision (RBS) data and any other data 
related to its financial health to the Stock Exchanges /Depositories. 
d) Failure to submit information sought by the Stock Exchange / 
Depositories on its dealing with related parties / promoters. 
e) Significant mark-to-market loss on proprietary account/ related party 
accounts 
f) Repeated instances of pay-in shortages. 
g) Significant trading exposure or amount of loans or advances given to 
and investments made in related parties/ group. 
h) Sudden activation of significant number of dormant client's accounts 
and / or significant activity in the dormant account/s. 
i) Significant number of UCC modifications. 
j) Resignation of Statutory Auditors or Directors. 
 
17.2.2 Early warning signals in relation to securities pledge transactions by the 
stock broker to be identified by the Depositories and shall be shared 
with Stock Exchanges which may include: 
a) Alerts for stock brokers maintaining multiple proprietary demat 
accounts and opening any new demat account in the name of stock 
                                                           
23 Reference: Circular SEBI/HO/MIRSD/DOP/CIR/P/2018/153 dated December 17,2018  
 
 
43  
 
 
broker for client purpose. 
b) Movement of shares to / from a large number of clients' demat 
accounts or large value shares to stock broker proprietary accounts 
and vice a versa. 
c) Transfer of large value of shares through off -market transfers other 
than for settlement purposes. 
d) Invocation of pledge of securities by lenders  against stock broker or 
his clients. 
e) Significant depletion of client's shares in the stock broker client 
account maintained by the stock broker. 
 
17.2.3 Increase in number of investor complaints against the stock 
broker/depository participant alleging un -authorized trading / 
unauthorized delivery instructions being processed and non -receipt of 
funds and securities and non-resolution of the same. 
 
17.2.4 Alerts generated from the monthly / weekly submissions made by stock 
broker under Risk Based Supervision (RBS) or Enhanced Supervision 
to the Stock Exchanges. 
a) Non-recovery of significant dues from debit balance clients over a 
period of time. 
b) Significant dues to credit balance clients over a period of time. 
c) Failure by stock broker to upload weekly data regarding monitoring 
of clients' funds as specified in SEBI's circular on Enhanced   
Supervision, for three consecutive weeks. 
d) Pledging securities in case of clients having credit balance and using 
the funds so  raised against them for own purposes or for funding 
debit balance of clients. 
e) Mis-reporting / wrong reporting about the client funds / securities. 
f) Significant increase in RBS score. 
17.2.5 Stock broker's terminal disabled for certain number of days in any 
segment / Stock Exchange in previous quarter. 
 
17.2.6 Stock Exchanges and Depositories shall frame an internal policy 
/guidelines regarding non-cooperation by stock brokers and depository 
participants during inspections which shall lay down the time period, the 
type of  documents critical for closing the inspections, which if not 
submitted, can be treated as non-cooperation. 
I. Failure to submit data sought for inspections especially relating to 
bank/demat accounts. client ledgers etc. despite repeated reminders. 
 
 
44  
 
 
II. Failure to  provide reasonable access to the records or any office 
premises. 
 
17.3 Stock Exchanges/ Clearing Corporations/ Depositories, shall devise a 
mechanism to detect diversion of clients' securities and to share information 
among themselves in respect of: 
 
17.3.1 Diversion of pay-out of securities to non-client accounts 
 
17.3.2 Mis-matches between gross (client-wise) securities pay-in and pay-out 
files of a stock brokers generated by the Clearing Corporation which 
shall be compared with actual transfer of securities to/from the cl ient's 
depository accounts by the Depository.  The cases of any mismatch 
found out by the Depository shall be informed to the concerned Stock 
Exchange / Clearing Corporation. 
  
17.3.3 Stock Exchange shall seek clarification from the concerned stock broker 
on the mismatches reported by Depository and identify transfer to a non-
client/third party, without any trade obligation. 
  
17.3.4 Such information on wrong / fraudulent / unauthorized transfer shall be 
shared by the Stock Exchange with other Stock Exchanges. 
 
17.4 Any other  alerts as the Stock Exchanges / Clearing Corporations and 
Depositories may deem fit. 
 
17.5 Alerts triggered at one Stock Exchange / Clearing Corporation/ Depository 
through early warning mechanism shall be immediately shared with other Stock 
Exchanges / Depositories with respect to the stock broker / depository 
participant. 
 
17.6 Based on the analysis of the early warning data, if it is established that the stock 
broker's financial health has deteriorated and/ or he has made unauthorized 
transfer of funds / se curities of the client, in such cases Stock Exchanges / 
Depositories shall jointly take preventive actions on the stock broker which may 
include one or more of, but not limited, to the following: 
 
17.6.1 Actions to be initiated by the Stock Exchanges like: 
17.6.1.1 Blocking of certain percentage of available collaterals 
towards margin. 
 
 
45  
 
 
 
17.6.1.2 Check securities register in respect of securities received 
and transferred against pay-in /pay-out against settlement  
 
17.6.1.3 Check details of funds and securities available with the 
clearing member, Clearing Corporation and the Depository 
of that stock broker. 
 
17.6.1.4 Impose limits on proprietary trading by the stock broker. 
 
17.6.1.5 Prescribe and monitor shorter time duration for settlement 
of Running Account of clients. 
 
17.6.1.6 Conduct meeting with the designated directors of the stock 
broker to seek appropriate explanation. 
 
17.6.1.7 Uniform action of deactivation of trading terminals by all 
Stock Exchanges based on the communication received 
from other Stock Exchange.  
 
17.6.1.8 Initiate inspection of the stock broker / depos itory 
participant. 
 
17.6.1.9 Cross check information submitted by stock broker with 
other independent sources like collateral details with the 
Clearing Corporation, transactions in Bank and 
Depositories, with statement collected directly etc. 
 
17.6.1.10 Where client money and securities diversion is suspected, 
appointed forensic auditor to trace trails of entire funds and 
securities of clients. 
 
17.6.2  Actions to be taken by the Depositories: 
 
17.6.2.1 Imposition of 100% concurrent audit on the depository 
participant. 
 
17.6.2.2 Cessation/ restriction on uses of Power of Attorney (POA) 
given to stock broker by clients mapped to such brokers 
only to meet settlement obligation of that client. Clients to 
 
 
46  
 
 
issue instructions electronically or through Delivery 
Instruction Slip (DIS) for delivery of shares  for off market 
transfers. 
 
17.6.3 Any other measures that Stock Exchanges/ Clearing Corporations/ 
Depositories may deem fit.

obligations.title: Cooperate with Early Warning Mechanism Inspections – Submit Required Documents
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must cooperate during inspections conducted by Stock Exchanges and Depositories. This includes submitting all data sought during inspections (especially relating to bank/demat accounts and client ledgers) without repeated reminders, and providing reasonable access to records and office premises. Non-cooperation, as defined in the internal policy/guidelines framed by Stock Exchanges and Depositories, may trigger early warning actions.
expected_controls: Documented inspection response procedure with designated responsible personnel
Centralised repository of bank/demat account records and client ledgers accessible for regulatory inspection
Policy for timely submission of inspection documents within prescribed timeframes
Physical/electronic access control procedures that permit regulator access to office premises and records

obligations.title: Timely Reporting of Financial Health Data to Stock Exchanges/Depositories
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must not delay the reporting of its Annual Report, Balance Sheet, Internal Audit Reports, Risk Based Supervision (RBS) data, and any other data related to its financial health to the Stock Exchanges and/or Depositories. The stock broker must also submit, without failure, information sought by Stock Exchanges/Depositories regarding its dealings with related parties and promoters.
expected_controls: Regulatory reporting calendar tracking all submission deadlines (Annual Report, Balance Sheet, Internal Audit, RBS data)
Escalation process for timely preparation and submission of related-party dealing disclosures
Internal review checklist to verify completeness of submissions before the due date
Alert mechanism for impending submission deadlines with accountability assigned to a senior officer

obligations.title: Monitor and Manage Net Worth and Financial Losses
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must monitor its financial health, including net worth and profitability, to detect significant reductions in net worth over the previous half-year/year and significant losses in previous half-years/years, as these constitute early warning signal triggers that may prompt regulatory scrutiny and preventive action.
expected_controls: Half-yearly and annual net worth computation and internal review process
Profit and loss monitoring against thresholds with escalation to senior management
Internal alert mechanism when net worth deterioration or significant losses are detected
Documentation of corrective measures taken when deterioration thresholds are breached

obligations.title: Monitor and Control Mark-to-Market Losses on Proprietary and Related-Party Accounts
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must monitor significant mark-to-market (MTM) losses on its proprietary account and related-party accounts, and must monitor the extent of trading exposure, loans, advances, and investments made in related parties/group entities, as these are early warning signal parameters that may indicate financial deterioration and trigger regulatory action.
expected_controls: Daily MTM computation for proprietary and related-party accounts with defined breach thresholds
Periodic review of loans, advances, and investments in related parties/group entities
Board/senior management reporting of related-party exposures breaching internal limits
Documented policy on permissible proprietary and related-party trading exposures

obligations.title: Prevent and Monitor Repeated Pay-in Shortages
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must take measures to prevent repeated instances of pay-in shortages, as such occurrences constitute an early warning signal of financial deterioration and may trigger regulatory scrutiny and preventive action by Stock Exchanges.
expected_controls: Pre-settlement monitoring of client and proprietary positions to ensure adequate securities for pay-in
Automated alerts for anticipated pay-in shortfalls prior to settlement deadlines
Root-cause analysis and corrective action process for each pay-in shortage occurrence
Internal reporting of pay-in shortage trends to compliance/risk management

obligations.title: Monitor Dormant Account Activations and UCC Modifications
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must monitor and have controls over the sudden activation of a significant number of dormant client accounts and/or significant activity in dormant accounts, as well as a significant number of Unique Client Code (UCC) modifications, as these are early warning signals that may indicate misuse of client accounts.
expected_controls: Automated alerts for sudden reactivation of multiple dormant client accounts within a defined period
Approval workflow and documentation requirements for UCC modifications
Threshold-based monitoring and reporting of dormant account activity to compliance
Periodic reconciliation of UCC modification records with client instructions

obligations.title: Report and Respond to Changes in Key Personnel – Resignation of Statutory Auditors or Directors
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must report and appropriately respond to the resignation of statutory auditors or directors, as such events constitute early warning signals of potential financial or governance deterioration and may trigger scrutiny from Stock Exchanges and Depositories.
expected_controls: Documented procedure for immediate regulatory notification upon resignation of statutory auditors or directors
Board-level review of circumstances leading to auditor/director resignations
Timely appointment of replacement auditors/directors in accordance with applicable law
Record-keeping of all communications related to auditor/director resignations

obligations.title: Upload Weekly Client Funds Monitoring Data Under Enhanced Supervision
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must upload weekly data regarding monitoring of clients' funds as specified in SEBI's circular on Enhanced Supervision, without failure for three or more consecutive weeks, as non-compliance is an early warning signal trigger that may result in regulatory action.
expected_controls: Weekly upload schedule for client funds monitoring data with automated reminders
Checklist and sign-off process confirming successful data upload each week
Escalation procedure when upload failures are detected
Record-keeping of upload confirmations and any exceptions with explanations

obligations.title: Ensure Accuracy of Client Funds and Securities Reporting – Avoid Mis-reporting
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must ensure that all reporting related to client funds and securities is accurate and must not mis-report or wrongly report data on client funds/securities in submissions made under Risk Based Supervision (RBS) or Enhanced Supervision, as mis-reporting is an early warning signal that may trigger regulatory action.
expected_controls: Independent review/checker process for client funds and securities data before submission
Reconciliation of RBS/Enhanced Supervision data against internal books and records prior to upload
Written attestation by responsible senior officer confirming accuracy of submissions
Audit trail and documentation of data sources used for regulatory submissions

obligations.title: Prohibit Pledging of Securities of Credit-Balance Clients for Own/Other Purposes
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must not pledge securities of clients who have a credit balance and use the funds so raised for the stock broker's own purposes or for funding the debit balance of other clients, as this constitutes an early warning signal and indicator of misuse of client assets.
expected_controls: System-level controls preventing pledge of securities belonging to credit-balance clients
Regular reconciliation to detect any pledge of credit-balance client securities
Policy prohibiting use of client pledged funds for proprietary or third-party client purposes
Periodic internal audit review of pledge transactions against client account balances

obligations.title: Recover Dues from Debit-Balance Clients and Settle Dues to Credit-Balance Clients Promptly
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must have processes to prevent non-recovery of significant dues from debit-balance clients over a period of time and must not carry significant outstanding dues to credit-balance clients over a period of time, as both conditions are early warning signals under Risk Based Supervision (RBS) monitoring.
expected_controls: Ageing analysis of debit-balance client dues with escalation and recovery procedures
Regular reporting and resolution process for significant credit-balance client dues
Threshold-based alerts for persistent debit/credit balance positions beyond defined periods
Internal audit review of client ledger balances at defined intervals

obligations.title: Avoid Prolonged Terminal Disablement Across Segments or Exchanges
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must ensure that its trading terminals are not disabled for a significant number of days in any segment or Stock Exchange in the previous quarter, as terminal disablement is an early warning signal that may trigger regulatory scrutiny and preventive actions.
expected_controls: Monitoring of trading terminal status across all segments and exchanges with documented uptime records
Escalation and resolution procedure for terminal disablement incidents
Root-cause analysis and corrective action for recurring terminal disablements
Internal compliance review of terminal operational status on a quarterly basis

obligations.title: Promptly Address Investor Complaints Relating to Unauthorized Trading and Non-Receipt of Funds/Securities
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must resolve investor complaints alleging unauthorized trading, unauthorized delivery instructions, and non-receipt of funds and securities in a timely manner. An increase in the number of unresolved such complaints is an early warning signal that may trigger preventive regulatory action.
expected_controls: Complaint tracking system with categorisation for unauthorized trading and funds/securities complaints
Defined turnaround times and escalation path for resolution of investor complaints
Periodic management review of pending and unresolved investor complaints
Root-cause analysis for recurring complaint categories with documented corrective action

obligations.title: Maintain Accurate Securities Register for Pay-in/Pay-out Against Settlement
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must maintain an accurate securities register recording all securities received and transferred against pay-in/pay-out related to settlement, enabling Stock Exchanges to verify the same in the event early warning signals are triggered.
expected_controls: Maintenance of a securities register recording all inward/outward movement of securities against settlement obligations
Daily reconciliation of securities register with depository account statements and clearing corporation records
Access controls ensuring integrity of securities register data
Periodic internal audit review of securities register accuracy

obligations.title: Comply with Shortened Running Account Settlement Timelines When Prescribed
obligations.obligation_text: When Stock Exchanges prescribe and monitor shorter time durations for the settlement of running accounts of clients (as a preventive action following early warning signal analysis), a stock broker must comply with the prescribed shorter settlement timelines for client running accounts.
expected_controls: Monitoring system to track running account settlement timelines per client
Operational process to execute running account settlements within any shortened timelines directed by Stock Exchanges
Compliance confirmation mechanism for each running account settlement cycle
Record-keeping of all running account settlements with dates and amounts

obligations.title: Cooperate with Stock Exchange Meetings Seeking Explanation on Financial Health
obligations.obligation_text: When a Stock Exchange conducts a meeting with the designated directors of a stock broker to seek explanation regarding deteriorating financial health or suspected diversion of client funds/securities, the stock broker's designated directors must cooperate and provide appropriate explanation.
expected_controls: Designation of directors responsible for attending regulatory inquiry meetings
Internal briefing process to prepare designated directors with accurate and documented responses
Record-keeping of all communications and minutes from meetings with regulators/exchanges
Legal/compliance review prior to regulatory meetings involving financial health inquiries

obligations.title: Comply with Trading Terminal Deactivation Orders from Stock Exchanges
obligations.obligation_text: A stock broker must comply with uniform deactivation of its trading terminals by all Stock Exchanges, where such deactivation is based on communication received from another Stock Exchange as part of preventive action following early warning signal analysis.
expected_controls: Documented procedure for immediate compliance with trading terminal deactivation orders
Communication protocol to all relevant internal teams upon receipt of deactivation instruction
Confirmation process to the issuing Stock Exchange that terminals have been deactivated
Record-keeping of all deactivation orders received and corresponding compliance actions

obligations.title: Cooperate with Forensic Audit When Client Money and Securities Diversion is Suspected
obligations.obligation_text: Where diversion of client money and securities is suspected, a stock broker must cooperate with a forensic auditor appointed by the Stock Exchange to trace the entire trail of client funds and securities, providing access to all relevant records and information.
expected_controls: Policy requiring full cooperation with forensic auditors including provision of bank, demat, and client records
Centralised, auditable records of all client fund and securities movements
Designated internal coordinator for managing forensic audit information requests
Legal/compliance review process for forensic audit cooperation procedures

obligations.title: Comply with Restrictions on Use of Client Power of Attorney to Settlement Obligations Only
obligations.obligation_text: When directed by a Depository as a preventive action, a stock broker must restrict the use of Power of Attorney (POA) granted by clients to the stock broker solely to meet the settlement obligation of the respective client. Off-market transfers must be executed only through client-issued electronic instructions or Delivery Instruction Slips (DIS), not through POA.
expected_controls: System controls limiting POA usage to settlement obligations only when directed by Depository
Process to obtain client-issued electronic instructions or DIS for all off-market transfers
Audit trail of all POA invocations with mapping to specific settlement obligations
Training for operations staff on restricted POA usage requirements

relations.source_note: successor master circular for stock brokers
